title: callista ophioglossa
intro: Callista ophioglossa, also known as the adder's tongue fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its roots, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Callista ophioglossa include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant can be dried and used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures that can be taken internally or applied topically to the affected area.
